label: C: Wide Slots (H=6144, S=256, N=1)
notes: Best final loss in the 5M sweep; strong but slower than baseline.

label: E: Baseline (H=4096, S=128, N=1) [current]
notes: Best efficiency in the 5M sweep; retained as the practical baseline reference.

label: B: 2-Expert (H=5888, S=128, N=2)
notes: Two-expert path improved loss over the baseline but paid a large step-time penalty.

label: D: 4-Expert (H=3072, S=128, N=4)
notes: Four experts underperformed and stayed expensive.

label: mtaps gate
notes: Best final loss in the top-5 architecture sweep.

label: mtaps current
notes: Best best-accuracy in the top-5 architecture sweep.

label: slot_dim=256
notes: Best non-MTAPS variant; supports wider slots as a meaningful ablation.

label: M=512
notes: Larger M helped only marginally relative to the baseline.

label: pilot pointer
notes: Pilot pointer regressed on this CPU top-5 sweep; kept as a task-dependent research variant.

label: BB fixed
notes: Bulletin-board fixed gate stayed close to baseline but did not justify the runtime cost.

label: BB learned
notes: Learned BB gate remained baseline-like; useful as a negative-control reference.

label: baseline
notes: Small CPU architecture sweep baseline; still the fastest and strongest of A/B/C.

label: wide+attn
notes: Wider+R2 attention variant underperformed the baseline while costing more.

label: wide_only
notes: Wider hidden-only variant also trailed the baseline on the same sweep.
